Massive open online courses
Open educational resources
Learning management system
triples.object: A learning management system ( LMS ) is a software application for the administration, documentation, tracking, reporting, automation, and delivery of educational courses, training programs, materials or learning and development programs. [ 1 ] The learning management system concept emerged directly

triples.subject: Flipped classroom
triples.object: A flipped classroom is an instructional strategy and a type of blended learning . It aims to increase student engagement and learning by having pupils complete readings at home, and work on live problem-solving during class time. [ 1 ] This pedagogical style moves activities, including those that ma

triples.subject: Open University
Coursera
triples.object: Coursera Inc. ( / k Ér Ë s É r É / ) is an American for-profit online university, earlier operating as a global massive open online course provider from 2012 until 2021. It was founded in 2012 [ 2 ] [ 3 ] by Stanford University computer science professors Andrew Ng and Daphne Koller . [ 4 ] Cour

triples.subject: EdX
triples.object: edX LLC is an American for-profit massive open online course provider. It was founded by MIT and Harvard . It is a subsidiary of 2U . [ 2 ]

triples.subject: FutureLearn
Digital divide
triples.object: Digital divide is unequal access to and use of digital technology , encompassing four interrelated dimensions: motivational, material, skills, and usage access. [ 1 ]

triples.subject: Learning analytics
triples.object: Learning analytics is the measurement, collection, analysis and reporting of data about learners and their contexts, for purposes of understanding and optimizing learning and the environments in which it occurs. [ 1 ] The growth of online learning since the 1990s, particularly in higher education ,

triples.subject: Adaptive learning
triples.object: Adaptive learning , also known as adaptive teaching , is an educational method which uses computer algorithms as well as artificial intelligence to orchestrate the interaction with the learner and deliver customized resources and learning activities to address the unique needs of each learner. [ 1 ]

triples.subject: Microlearning
triples.object: Microlearning refers to a set of compact e-learning modules that are designed to reduce learner fatigue. The modules can be educational, professional, or skill-based , and are usually designed to be less than 20 minutes long, with a single learning objective or topic. [ 1 ] The name originates from

triples.subject: Peer learning
triples.object: One of the most visible approaches to peer learning comes out of cognitive psychology , and is applied within a "mainstream" educational framework: "Peer learning is an educational practice in which students interact with other students to attain educational goals." [ 1 ] Other authors including Dav

triples.subject: Blended learning
triples.object: Blended learning or hybrid learning , also known as technology-mediated instruction , web-enhanced instruction , or mixed-mode instruction , is an approach to education that combines online educational materials and opportunities for interaction online with physical place-based classroom methods.

triples.subject: Online assessment
triples.object: Electronic assessment , also known as digital assessment , e-assessment , online assessment or computer-based assessment , is the use of information technology in assessment such as educational assessment , health assessment , psychiatric assessment , and psychological assessment . This covers a wid

triples.subject: Personal learning network
triples.object: A Personal Learning Network (PLN) is an informal learning network that consists of the people a learner interacts with and derives knowledge from in a personal learning environment . In a PLN, a person makes a connection with another person with the specific intent that some type of learning will oc

triples.subject: Online collaboration
triples.object: Computer-supported cooperative work ( CSCW ) or computer-supported collaboration is the study of how people utilize technology collaboratively, often towards a shared goal. [ 1 ] CSCW addresses how computer systems can support collaborative activity and coordination. [ 2 ] More specifically, the fie

triples.subject: Massive online open research
triples.object: Massive online open research ( MOOR ) is an online research and development (R&D) open access platform or higher education study program aiming at unlimited participation via the internet.

triples.subject: Georgia Tech Online Master of Science in Computer Science
triples.object: Georgia Tech Online Master of Science in Computer Science (OMSCS) is a Master of Science degree offered by the College of Computing at Georgia Tech . The program was launched in 2014 in partnership with Udacity and AT&T and delivered through the massive open online course (MOOC) format. [ 2 ] Georgi

triples.subject: Language MOOC
triples.object: Language MOOCs ( Language Massive Open Online Courses , or LMOOCs ) are web-based online courses freely accessible for a limited period of time, created for those interested in developing their skills in a foreign language. As Sokolik (2014) [ 1 ] states, enrolment is large, free and not restricted

triples.subject: George Siemens
triples.object: George Siemens is a Canadian expatriate professor of psychology at the University of Texas at Arlington [ 1 ] [ 2 ] and professor and director of the Centre for Change and Complexity in Learning [ 3 ] at the University of South Australia. [ 4 ] He is known for his theory of connectivism , which seek

triples.subject: Mushtak Al-Atabi
triples.object: Mushtak Al-Atabi is the provost and CEO of Heriot Watt University Malaysia. [ 1 ] His research focuses on thermofluids, renewable energy, biomechanical engineering, and engineering education. He is an Honorary Chair at the School of Mechanical Engineering of the University of Birmingham (UK) and the

triples.subject: Iversity
triples.object: Iversity (stylised as "iversity") is a Berlin-based online education platform. Since October 2013, iversity has specialised in providing online courses and lectures in higher education , specifically MOOCs ( Massive Open Online Courses ). [ 1 ] [ 2 ] [ 3 ] Courses are free and open for anyone to enr

triples.subject: MITx
triples.object: MITx is the massive open online course (MOOC) program at Massachusetts Institute of Technology .  A constituent program of MIT's Office of Digital Learning, MITx produces MOOCs from MIT departments and faculty.  Prior to 2U's acquisition of edX , MITx courses appeared on edX.  After the acquisition,

triples.subject: OTEN
triples.object: The Open Training and Education Network , often abbreviated OTEN , is an Open education service offered by TAFE NSW . It was rebranded as TAFE Digital during the OneTAFE restructure at the beginning of 2018. Students may enrol from anywhere in Australia, while operationally the program is run by the

triples.subject: National Programme on Technology Enhanced Learning
triples.object: The National Programme on Technology Enhanced Learning (NPTEL) is an Indian e-learning platform for university-level science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) subjects. NPTEL is the largest e-repository in the world of courses in engineering, basic sciences and selected humanities and

triples.subject: P2PU
triples.object: Peer 2 Peer University ( P2PU ) is a nonprofit online open learning community which allows users to organize and participate in courses and study groups to learn about specific topics. Peer 2 Peer University was started in 2009 with funding from the Hewlett Foundation and the Shuttleworth Foundation

triples.subject: D2L
triples.object: D2L (or Desire2Learn ) is a Canada -based global software company with offices in Australia , Brazil , Europe , India , Singapore , and the United States .

triples.subject: List of MOOC providers
triples.object: The following is a list of notable massive open online course providers (MOOCs) worldwide.

triples.subject: MIT OpenCourseWare
triples.object: MIT OpenCourseWare ( MIT OCW ) is an initiative of the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) to publish all of the educational materials from its undergraduate - and graduate-level courses online, freely and openly available to anyone, anywhere. The project was announced on April 4, 2001, [ 1

triples.subject: Centre for Law and Democracy
triples.object: The Centre for Law and Democracy ( CLD ) is a non-profit organisation based in Halifax, Nova Scotia, Canada. The CLD works worldwide to promote, protect, and develop those human rights that underpin democracy, including the right to information (RTI), freedom of expression , freedom of the press , f

triples.subject: Online learning community
triples.object: An online learning community is a public or private destination on the Internet that addresses its members' learning needs by facilitating peer-to-peer learning. Through social networking and computer-mediated communication , or the use of datagogies while people work as a community to achieve a sha

triples.subject: Fast.ai
triples.object: fast.ai is a non-profit research group focused on deep learning and artificial intelligence . It was founded in 2016 by Jeremy Howard and Rachel Thomas with the goal of democratizing deep learning. [ 1 ] They do this by providing a massive open online course (MOOC) named "Practical Deep Learning for

triples.subject: Frank van Diggelen
triples.object: Frank Stephen Tromp van Diggelen [ 1 ] is a Distinguished Engineer at Google . His work concerns GPS / GNSS navigation. He helped to create some of the first GPS chips used in commercial smartphones. Van Diggelen is a fellow of the IEEE , Institute of Navigation and Royal Institute of Navigation .

triples.subject: OpenHPI (Online Education)
triples.object: openHPI is a platform for massive open online courses ( MOOC ) in the field of computer science and information technology. It is hosted at the Hasso Plattner Institute (HPI) in Potsdam, Germany. [ 1 ] openHPI is open to everyone, and participation is free of charge. Everybody can register and enrol

triples.subject: List of online educational resources
triples.object: This is a list of online education platforms such as open source , online university , and proprietary platforms.

triples.subject: Online learning in higher education
triples.object: Online learning involves courses offered by primary institutions that are 100% virtual. Online learning, or virtual classes offered over the internet, is contrasted with traditional courses taken in a brick-and-mortar school building.
